criteria_verification.criterion_text: Group A (6 key examples) — all pass

criteria_verification.notes: All 6 Group A scenarios (Queueing-creates-pending-build, full-lifecycle-to-completion, Status-shows-active-and-recent, History-lists-prior-attempts, Defaults-and-overrides, Status-responsive-during-write) pass against the production persistence + state_machine modules.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Group B (6 boundary conditions) — all pass; Scenario Outlines fan
criteria_verification.evidence: Group B fans out as expected: turn-budget-accepted [1, 5] (2 rows), turn-budget-rejected [0, -1] (2 rows), history-limit [(10,50,10),(60,50,50),(60,1,1)] (3 rows), default-history-50, duplicate-correlation-refused, full-status-five-stages — all pass. Outlines visible in pytest output as e.g. test_reasoningturn_budget_overrides_at_or_above_the_minimum_are_accepted[1].
criteria_verification.notes: Group B fans out as expected: turn-budget-accepted [1, 5] (2 rows), turn-budget-rejected [0, -1] (2 rows), history-limit [(10,50,10),(60,50,50),(60,1,1)] (3 rows), default-history-50, duplicate-correlation-refused, full-status-five-stages — all pass. Outlines visible in pytest output as e.g. test_reasoningturn_budget_overrides_at_or_above_the_minimum_are_accepted[1].

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Group C (7 negative cases) — all pass
criteria_verification.evidence: All 7 Group C scenarios (path-allowlist-refused, in-flight-duplicate, skip-on-non-paused, cancel-of-unknown, validation-fail, hard-stop-fail, invalid-transition) pass. The path-allowlist and in-flight scenarios assert exit_code == EXIT_PATH_REFUSED / EXIT_DUPLICATE; the validation/hard-stop/invalid-transition scenarios route through state_machine.transition with InvalidTransitionError raised on illegal moves.
criteria_verification.notes: All 7 Group C scenarios (path-allowlist-refused, in-flight-duplicate, skip-on-non-paused, cancel-of-unknown, validation-fail, hard-stop-fail, invalid-transition) pass. The path-allowlist and in-flight scenarios assert exit_code == EXIT_PATH_REFUSED / EXIT_DUPLICATE; the validation/hard-stop/invalid-transition scenarios route through state_machine.transition with InvalidTransitionError raised on illegal moves.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Group D (9 edge cases) — all pass; crash recovery scenarios use
criteria_verification.evidence: All 9 Group D scenarios (crash-during-prep, crash-during-running, crash-during-finalising, paused-survives-crash, terminal-states-after-crash x4, cancel-paused, skip-flagged, sequential-queue, watch-status) pass. The four crash-recovery scenarios run forge.lifecycle.recovery.reconcile_on_boot against a seeded SQLite state (PREPARING/RUNNING/FINALISING/PAUSED rows seeded via _seed_build) and assert the per-state action matrix from API-sqlite-schema.md §6.
criteria_verification.notes: All 9 Group D scenarios (crash-during-prep, crash-during-running, crash-during-finalising, paused-survives-crash, terminal-states-after-crash x4, cancel-paused, skip-flagged, sequential-queue, watch-status) pass. The four crash-recovery scenarios run forge.lifecycle.recovery.reconcile_on_boot against a seeded SQLite state (PREPARING/RUNNING/FINALISING/PAUSED rows seeded via _seed_build) and assert the per-state action matrix from API-sqlite-schema.md §6.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Group E (2 security) — path-traversal includes URL-encoded variants
criteria_verification.evidence: Both Group E scenarios pass: path-traversal exercises validate_feature_id (which decodes %2F / double-encoded variants per identifiers.py) and asserts exit_code == EXIT_INVALID_IDENTIFIER; cancel-operator-distinct seeds a build with originating_user='alice' and asserts the originator is preserved while the cancelling-operator (responder='bob') is recorded on the resolution.
criteria_verification.notes: Both Group E scenarios pass: path-traversal exercises validate_feature_id (which decodes %2F / double-encoded variants per identifiers.py) and asserts exit_code == EXIT_INVALID_IDENTIFIER; cancel-operator-distinct seeds a build with originating_user='alice' and asserts the originator is preserved while the cancelling-operator (responder='bob') is recorded on the resolution.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Group F (2 concurrency) — uses two threads invoking `forge queue`
criteria_verification.evidence: Both Group F scenarios pass: two-features-queued-concurrently exercises back-to-back record_pending_build calls and asserts distinct build_ids and queue-time ordering preserved; concurrent-reader spawns a threading.Thread that calls read_history while the main thread writes a stage_log row, and asserts the reader sees a consistent snapshot. The connect_writer / read_only_connect helpers apply WAL-mode pragmas (DDR-003) so this concurrency exercise is meaningful.
criteria_verification.notes: Both Group F scenarios pass: two-features-queued-concurrently exercises back-to-back record_pending_build calls and asserts distinct build_ids and queue-time ordering preserved; concurrent-reader spawns a threading.Thread that calls read_history while the main thread writes a stage_log row, and asserts the reader sees a consistent snapshot. The connect_writer / read_only_connect helpers apply WAL-mode pragmas (DDR-003) so this concurrency exercise is meaningful.

criteria_verification.evidence: Both Group G scenarios pass: terminal-completion-time fans out across [complete, failed, cancelled, skipped] and asserts completed_at is non-NULL plus no further transitions are permitted (compose_transition raises InvalidTransitionError for any other target); write-then-publish-failure flips stub_publisher.raise_on_publish=True, invokes forge queue, and asserts exit_code == EXIT_PUBLISH_FAILED with the build still visible as QUEUED in SQLite.
criteria_verification.notes: Both Group G scenarios pass: terminal-completion-time fans out across [complete, failed, cancelled, skipped] and asserts completed_at is non-NULL plus no further transitions are permitted (compose_transition raises InvalidTransitionError for any other target); write-then-publish-failure flips stub_publisher.raise_on_publish=True, invokes forge queue, and asserts exit_code == EXIT_PUBLISH_FAILED with the build still visible as QUEUED in SQLite.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Group H (1 integration boundary) — NATS unreachable + `forge status`
criteria_verification.evidence: Group H 'messaging-unreachable' scenario flips stub_publisher.raise_on_publish=True, invokes forge queue (asserts EXIT_PUBLISH_FAILED + 'messaging-layer' in stderr), then verifies persistence.read_status() returns without touching the publish seam — proving the SQLite read path is functional even with the messaging layer down.
criteria_verification.notes: Group H 'messaging-unreachable' scenario flips stub_publisher.raise_on_publish=True, invokes forge queue (asserts EXIT_PUBLISH_FAILED + 'messaging-layer' in stderr), then verifies persistence.read_status() returns without touching the publish seam — proving the SQLite read path is functional even with the messaging layer down.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: No real NATS connections are made by the test suite (CI-friendly)
criteria_verification.evidence: forge.cli.queue.publish is monkey-patched onto _PsmStubPipelinePublisher.publish via the forge_runner fixture; recovery.reconcile_on_boot is invoked with stub_failure_publisher and stub_approval_publisher. No nats.connect() is ever called.
criteria_verification.notes: forge.cli.queue.publish is monkey-patched onto _PsmStubPipelinePublisher.publish via the forge_runner fixture; recovery.reconcile_on_boot is invoked with stub_failure_publisher and stub_approval_publisher. No nats.connect() is ever called.

issues.description:
Advisory (non-blocking): task-work produced a report with 2 of 3 expected agent invocations. Missing phases: 3 (Implementation). Consider invoking these agents via the Task tool to strengthen stack-specific quality:
- Phase 3: `python-api-specialist` (Implementation)

rationale: All quality gates passed. Tests not required for this task type. All acceptance criteria met. Architecture context: 340 chars provided.
context_used:
## Job-Specific Context

Budget: 1638/5200 tokens

### 🎨 Relevant Patterns
- IS_USED_FOR
- CREATED_AT
- USED_FOR
- ASSOCIATED_WITH

### ⚠️ Warnings
- DEPENDS_ON
- REQUIRES_USE_OF
- ERROR_CAUSE_OF

### 🎭 Role Constraints
- DELEGATE_TO_TASK_WORK
- IS_CREATED_FOR
- REQUIRES_ASK_BEFORE
- MUST_PERFORM

### 🛠️ Implementation Modes
- APPLIES_TO
